detail: Loose requires at least one side (or AB) to declare this.
Both sides or AB canonical interface required.

A_value: A.in:melt_rate; sub-shelf melt-rate fields provided externally e.g.; from isomip+ runs; sub-shelf melt rates used as forcing | B.in:ice_draft_geometry; grounding_line_position; ice geometry from an ice-sheet model run e.g.; mismip+; ice geometry to drive ocean cavity model
2d or 3d flow approximations ssa, sia, hybrid, or full-stokes; prescribed bed topography, accumulation fields; synthetic ice shelf geometry
B_value: ice draft geometry, grounding line provided externally; no atmospheric or sea-ice interactions; idealized far-field ocean restoring
AB_value: shared topography, domain between ice, ocean components; ocean-induced melting is a dominant driver of grounding-line retreat; simplified melt rate parameterization at the ice-ocean interface; no external atmosphere or sea ice forcing; idealized bedrock, open-ocean boundary conditions

A_value: idealized benchmark experiments to evaluate marine ice sheet model behavior, with a focus on grounding-line dynamics, buttressing under varying sub-shelf melt rates. used here as the ice component that consumes ocean-driven melt rates, produces updated ice geometry.; sub-shelf melt-rate fields provided externally e.g., from isomip+ runs; topography, ice rheology, sliding laws; sub-shelf melt rates used as forcing
B_value: ocean-only benchmark experiments simulating circulation, melt beneath idealized ice shelves. used here as the ocean component that consumes ice geometry, produces sub-shelf melt rates.; ice geometry from an ice-sheet model run e.g., mismip+; thermodynamics, drag coefficients, far-field profiles; ice geometry to drive ocean cavity model
AB_value: a modular modeling framework that integrates marine ice sheet models mismip+ with regional ocean circulation models isomip+ to simulate the coupled dynamics of ice sheet retreat, ocean-induced basal melting. misomip1 is designed for idealized domains resembling the amundsen sea embayment to assess the feedbacks between ocean circulation, grounding-line migration under varying thermal conditions.; prescribed far-field ocean profiles, melt parameterizations, coupling time steps; initial, evolving ice draft, grounding-line geometry; sub-shelf melt rates, ocean feedbacks

B_value: simulate sub-shelf melt rates in response to thermal forcing using supplied ice geometry; consumes ice draft, grounding-line fields to compute melt
